page: Microsoft Azure E96as_v4 is an EASv4-series virtual machine configured with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 7.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor operating at 3.525 GHz, the instance features 48 physical cores with multithreading enabled under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The architecture is x86_64, and the hardware includes a local storage allocation of 1376 GB with Premium Storage capability. No GPUs are bundled with this server configuration.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance ranking in the top 10%. Passmark memory benchmarks and CPU encryption tests also place in the top 10%, reflecting high cryptographic throughput and memory subsystem efficiency. Single-core execution, measured by stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core tests, falls into the top 25% and middle 50% respectively. Large-block memory bandwidth read and write operations perform at average levels, while small-block cached reads achieve strong top-25% throughput.

The high memory density and strong multi-threaded performance make this instance suitable for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and large-scale data processing. Passmark database operations and Redis SET benchmarks both demonstrate strong top-25% performance, supporting its utility for relational databases and in-memory caching. While the server handles small-model LLM inference efficiently, processing speeds for larger models drop to average levels, highlighting a performance tradeoff for complex AI workloads without dedicated GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure E96as_v4 is an EASv4-series virtual machine designed for memory-intensive workloads. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs and 672.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz across 48 physical cores. The instance includes 1376 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability and operates on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory scores, alongside strong cryptographic and database execution. However, large-block memory bandwidth and single-core processing remain average. This resource profile is highly suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and multi-threaded enterprise applications, offering a balanced tradeoff between high memory capacity and robust multi-core compute density without the inclusion of dedicated GPU hardware.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96as_v4 is an EASv4 virtual machine featuring 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and 1376 GB local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance for databases.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96as_v4 is a memory-optimized VM with 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and 1376 GB local storage for database and enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity, memory-optimized virtual machine featuring ninety-six AMD EPYC vCPUs and local storage for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 vCPUs and 672 GB of system memory
1376 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory performance in benchmarks
Strong database operations and Redis SET throughput
Designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and databases
